summary: Updated the native swarm manager loop so an objective with zero tasks no longer reports `objective-complete`. `manager next` now returns an actionable `empty` / `plan-tasks` response with a `claspc swarm task create ...` suggested command, and added regressions covering both the control-plane JSON and text rendering.
residual_risks: Only the focused swarm Rust tests were run; broader native/runtime or repo-wide verification was not run per task instructions.

The worktree already had unrelated changes/untracked files outside this edit (`src/.native-verify.lock.d/pid`, `src/native-verify/promoted.source.check.json`, `.clasp-loops/`, and a native verify cache entry).

feedback.summary: This closes a real manager-loop bug: empty objectives now stay actionable instead of falsely reading as complete.
ergonomics: `manager next` now aligns with `projectedObjectiveStatus = empty`, which makes the control signal coherent for live steering.
The suggested command gives the manager an immediate next step instead of falling back to a misleading completed state.
follow_ups: Consider adding a dedicated manager action for expanding/planning an empty objective into an initial DAG, rather than only suggesting raw `task create`.
Consider a focused CLI integration test for `claspc swarm manager next --json` once there is a stable native command harness for these flows.
warnings: I did not run `bash scripts/verify-all.sh`, per the task instructions.
The `nix develop` test runs emitted existing compiler/runtime warnings unrelated to this change.
